stories.title: Claude 4.6 Opus Released with 1M Token Context and Native Tool Use
stories.snippet: Anthropic releases Claude 4.6 Opus with 1 million token context window and built-in tool use capabilities.

stories.title: EU Passes Comprehensive AI Liability Directive
stories.snippet: The European Parliament votes to hold AI companies liable for damages caused by their systems.

stories.title: Mistral Raises $2B at $15B Valuation
stories.snippet: French AI startup Mistral closes massive funding round, becoming Europe's most valuable AI company.

stories.title: LLaMA 4 Scout Open-Sourced with 10M Context Window
stories.snippet: Meta releases LLaMA 4 Scout under Apache 2.0, featuring a 10 million token context window.

stories.title: Scaling Laws Paper Shows Diminishing Returns Above 1T Parameters
stories.snippet: New research suggests that scaling language models beyond 1 trillion parameters yields minimal improvements in reasoning tasks.

stories.title: Major Deepfake Scandal Hits Political Campaign in US Midterms
stories.snippet: AI-generated deepfake video of a Senate candidate goes viral, raising questions about election integrity.

stories.title: Apple Integrates On-Device LLM into iOS 20 Spotlight Search
stories.snippet: Apple ships a 3B parameter language model that runs entirely on-device for Spotlight search and Siri.

stories.title: OpenAI and Google Agree to Joint Safety Testing Framework
stories.snippet: The two leading AI labs announce a shared framework for testing frontier model safety before deployment.

stories.title: Hugging Face Reaches 1 Million Public Models
stories.snippet: The open-source ML platform crosses a historic milestone with one million publicly available models.

stories.title: Study: AI-Written Code Has 3x More Security Vulnerabilities
stories.snippet: Stanford researchers find that code generated by LLMs contains significantly more security flaws than human-written code.

beats.headline: Claude 4.6 Opus: 1M Tokens, Native Tools
beats.narration: In our top story this week, Anthropic has released Claude 4.6 Opus with a one million token context window and built-in tool use. This is the largest context window of any commercially available model, and it changes how developers build AI applications.

beats.headline: EU Passes AI Liability Directive
beats.narration: Moving on to regulation. The European Parliament has voted to hold AI companies directly liable for damages caused by their systems. This is the most significant AI legislation since the AI Act, and it could reshape how tech companies operate in Europe.

beats.headline: Mistral Raises $2B, Valued at $15B
beats.narration: In funding news, French startup Mistral has closed a two billion dollar round at a fifteen billion dollar valuation. That makes them Europe's most valuable AI company, and a serious challenger to the American giants.

beats.headline: LLaMA 4 Scout: 10M Context, Open Source
beats.narration: The open source world got a gift this week. Meta has released LLaMA 4 Scout under Apache 2.0 with a ten million token context window. That is ten times what most commercial models offer, and it is free.

beats.headline: Scaling Laws Hit a Wall at 1T Parameters
beats.narration: Turning to research. A new paper suggests that scaling language models beyond one trillion parameters yields diminishing returns. If confirmed, this could fundamentally shift how the industry invests its compute budgets.

beats.headline: Deepfake Scandal Rocks US Midterms
beats.narration: In a concerning development, an AI-generated deepfake video of a US Senate candidate went viral this week. The video was viewed millions of times before being flagged, raising urgent questions about election integrity.

beats.headline: Apple Ships On-Device LLM in iOS 20
beats.narration: Apple has quietly shipped a three billion parameter language model that runs entirely on your iPhone. Spotlight search and Siri now use it for natural language understanding — no cloud required.

beats.headline: OpenAI and Google Agree on Safety Testing
beats.narration: In a rare show of cooperation, OpenAI and Google have agreed to a joint safety testing framework for frontier models. Both companies will now test each other's models before public deployment.

beats.headline: AI Code Has 3x More Security Flaws
beats.narration: And finally, a Stanford study found that code generated by large language models contains three times more security vulnerabilities than human-written code. Something to think about the next time you accept that autocomplete suggestion.
